summary: All three rows are 'companion-already-filed-and-already-counted' artifacts: each EX-99.1 announces a period that the prior periodic snapshot (or an earlier 8-K) had already covered, so D's 'advance prior periodic by one quarter' rule produces a label one quarter ahead of truth. Concretely: PHR is a transcript-only follow-up 8-K filed 4 days after the real Q4 8-K; PINC is a same-quarter earnings 8-K filed one day after the Q3 10-Q (10-Q-before-8-K ordering); PRU is a recast Q4 2025 quarterly financial supplement (segmentation-changes restatement) bundled with preliminary Q1 2026 commentary. The failure-model claim that prior-periodic advance is coherent but the issuer chose not to advance is REFINED, not refuted.

per_row.ex99_quote: Phreesia, Inc. Q4 Fiscal 2026 Earnings Call Transcript (March 30, 2026) ... Good evening and welcome to Phreesia's earnings conference call for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026, which ended on January 31 of 2026.
per_row.filing_items_8k: Item 2.02 (Results of Operations and Financial Condition); Item 9.01 (Financial Statements and Exhibits). Period of Report: 2026-03-30

companion_periodic.cover_quote: For the fiscal year ended January 31, 2026

per_row.what_actually_happened: PHR filed the real Q4 FY2026 earnings 8-K on 2026-03-30 (accession 0001412408-26-000074, also in fact sheet — D handled it correctly there). The 10-K followed on 2026-03-31. Then on 2026-04-03 PHR filed THIS additional 8-K furnishing only the earnings-call transcript as EX-99.1 for the SAME Q4 FY2026 period that was already announced. By the time D evaluates this transcript-only 8-K, its 'prior periodic snapshot' is the FY2026 10-K (Q4/FY), so D dutifully advances Q4→Q1 of FY2027. But the 8-K is not announcing Q1 FY2027 — it is re-furnishing the just-completed Q4 FY2026 earnings call.
per_row.evidence_quote_for_class: Filename literal: 'phr-ex991q4fy26earningstra.htm'. EX-99.1 first words: 'Phreesia, Inc. Q4 Fiscal 2026 Earnings Call Transcript (March 30, 2026)'. CFO opening remarks: 'welcome to Phreesia's earnings conference call for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026, which ended on January 31 of 2026.'
per_row.structural_signal_available: STRONG. (1) EX-99.1 filename contains 'earningstra' / 'transcript'; (2) the 8-K is filed AFTER the same issuer's 10-K (the Q4 10-K is at filed=2026-03-31 covering 2026-01-31, this 8-K is 2026-04-03); (3) there is an immediately preceding 8-K (accession 0001412408-26-000074, filed 2026-03-30, period_of_report 2026-03-30) for the same issuer with Item 2.02 + EX-99.1 'phr-ex991q4fy26.htm'; (4) the new 8-K's period_of_report (2026-03-30) MATCHES the prior earnings 8-K's period_of_report (2026-03-30) exactly — a duplicate period_of_report within ~1 week is a near-deterministic 'transcript supplement' signal.

per_row.ex99_quote: Premier, Inc. Reports Fiscal-Year 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results ... Premier, Inc. (NASDAQ: PINC) ... today reported financial results for the fiscal-year 2025 third quarter ended March 31, 2025.
per_row.filing_items_8k: Item 2.02 (Results of Operations and Financial Condition); Item 7.01 (Regulation FD Disclosure); Item 9.01 (Financial Statements and Exhibits). Period of Report: 2025-05-06

companion_periodic.cover_quote: For the quarterly period ended March 31, 2025

per_row.what_actually_happened: Premier files the 10-Q FIRST (2025-05-06) and the earnings 8-K SECOND (2025-05-07) — the inverse of the typical sequence where the press-release 8-K precedes the 10-Q. Both documents cover the SAME quarter (Q3 FY2025, period 2025-03-31). When D resolves the 8-K, the prior-periodic snapshot already contains the Q3 FY2025 10-Q. D therefore treats Q3 as 'prior' and projects forward to Q4, but the 8-K is the press-release companion to that very same Q3 10-Q. PINC's earlier rows in the fact sheet (rows 2-4, 8) used `rule_g_strict_direct_recent_prior_calendar` and got identical same-day-prior 10-Qs right; here D fell into the projection branch instead. The trigger appears to be that the 10-Q was filed strictly BEFORE midnight on the prior calendar day (filed 2025-05-06 vs. 8-K filed 2025-05-07), so the resolver classified it as 'prior periodic' rather than 'direct recent companion.'
per_row.mechanism_subclass: 10-Q-before-8-K filing inversion (companion is for SAME quarter, but filed strictly before the 8-K's calendar day)
per_row.evidence_quote_for_class: EX-99.1: 'today reported financial results for the fiscal-year 2025 third quarter ended March 31, 2025.' Companion 10-Q period_of_report = 2025-03-31, xbrl_q = Q3 (same quarter as EX-99.1). Filing dates: 10-Q 2025-05-06, 8-K 2025-05-07.
per_row.structural_signal_available: STRONG. The decisive signal is 'companion 10-Q period_of_report is the SAME quarter the EX-99.1 will turn out to announce' — but D doesn't read EX-99.1. Without EX-99.1, the available signal is 'companion 10-Q exists with period_of_report within ~7 days BEFORE the 8-K, AND that period falls inside the typical 8-K reporting cadence for that issuer' — i.e., the 10-Q-before-8-K inversion is itself a learnable pattern. PINC has done this consistently (2024-02-06/07, 2024-05-07/08, 2024-11-05/06, 2025-02-04/05, 2025-05-06/07). A per-issuer 'inversion habit' flag could detect this. Also: the 8-K period_of_report (2025-05-06) matches the 10-Q filing date, not a typical earnings-event date — another weak signal.

per_row.ex99_quote: Prudential Financial, Inc. (PRU) As Adjusted for Segmentation Changes Quarterly Financial Supplement Fourth Quarter 2025 ... Effective January 1, 2026, the Company made the following segment reporting changes ... The updates made for these revisions are highlighted in yellow throughout Exhibit 99.1.
per_row.filing_items_8k: Item 2.02 (Results of Operations and Financial Condition); Item 7.01 (Regulation FD Disclosure); Item 9.01 (Financial Statements and Exhibits). Period of Report: 2026-04-14. NOTE: NOT an Item 4.02 non-reliance — this is a voluntary recasting under Item 2.02/7.01.

companion_periodic.note: Fact sheet companion fields are EMPTY for this row — no companion 10-Q/10-K was filed alongside this 8-K because the 8-K is a recast supplement, not a new earnings event with a new periodic.

per_row.what_actually_happened: Prudential's 8-K body says: 'Prudential Financial, Inc. ... is furnishing this Current Report to disclose certain (1) preliminary financial information regarding the quarter ended March 31, 2026, prior to the availability of the Company's quarterly earnings release and quarterly financial supplement for the period, scheduled for release on May 5, 2026; and (2) segment reporting changes.' The EX-99.1 attached is the Quarterly Financial Supplement for FOURTH QUARTER 2025 'As Adjusted for Segmentation Changes' — i.e., a recast of already-reported Q4 2025 numbers under the new segmentation effective 2026-01-01. The audit truth (Tier C) was assigned 2025 Q4 because EX-99.1's dominant header is '4Q25.' The 8-K is genuinely multi-period (preliminary Q1 2026 metrics + recast Q4 2025 supplement); the audit chose Q4 2025. D's 'FY2025 Q4 prior + advance one quarter -> FY2026 Q1' reasoning matches the body's preliminary Q1 2026 numbers but does NOT match the EX-99.1-driven Tier C assignment. Same pattern recurred in row 6 (accession 0001137774-25-000013, EX-99.1 'exhibit991-3q24qfsrestatem.htm', also wrong by D, also Tier C, also q3_to_q4 advance) — i.e., this is a repeated Prudential-specific 'recast supplement 8-K' habit.
per_row.mechanism_subclass: voluntary recast / re-issued QFS under Item 2.02 (NOT Item 4.02 non-reliance) — bundled with preliminary next-quarter commentary
per_row.evidence_quote_for_class: Filename literal: 'exhibit991-4q25qfsrestatem.htm' ('qfsrestatem' = Quarterly Financial Supplement RESTATEMENT). EX-99.1 header: 'As Adjusted for Segmentation Changes Quarterly Financial Supplement Fourth Quarter 2025'. EX-99.1 prose: 'Effective January 1, 2026, the Company made the following segment reporting changes ... The updates made for these revisions are highlighted in yellow throughout Exhibit 99.1.' 8-K body: 'is furnishing this Current Report to disclose certain (1) preliminary financial information regarding the quarter ended March 31, 2026 ... and (2) segment reporting changes.'
per_row.structural_signal_available: MODERATE. Available signals: (1) EX-99.1 filename contains 'restatem' / 'qfsrestat' (a Prudential-specific naming convention — not portable across issuers); (2) NO companion periodic 10-Q/10-K is filed alongside or just after this 8-K (companion fields are empty in the fact sheet); (3) the issuer ALREADY filed a Q4 2025 earnings 8-K (accession 0001137774-26-000012, filed 2026-02-03) and a 10-K (accession 0001137774-26-000048, filed 2026-02-12) BEFORE this 8-K — so 'prior earnings 8-K already covers the same fiscal period AND a 10-K already exists for that fiscal year' is a detectable structural fingerprint. (4) Repeated Prudential pattern: the 2025-01-15 row 6 (accession ...0001137774-25-000013, '3q24qfsrestatem') has the same shape — a per-issuer 'recast supplement' habit could be learned. Items 2.02/7.01/9.01 alone do NOT distinguish recast from regular earnings.

verdict_explanation: The failure-model claim — 'prior periodic projection metadata says advancing is coherent, but the current 8-K event chose not to advance because the issuer skipped a quarter or is announcing a non-standard period' — is directionally correct but each of the three rows is a DIFFERENT non-standard-period mechanism, none of which is a true 'skipped quarter' (F4): (a) PHR is a transcript-only re-furnishing of an already-filed earnings 8-K (F6 / transcript supplement) — the issuer didn't skip Q1, the 8-K just isn't a new period announcement at all; (b) PINC is a 10-Q-filed-before-8-K timing inversion where the companion 10-Q covers the SAME quarter as the 8-K (F5 / direct-recent same-quarter), so D's 'advance' is wrong in both directions — there is no advance, the periodic and the 8-K are paired for the same Q3 FY2025; (c) PRU is a voluntary recast of an already-announced Q4 2025 quarterly financial supplement under Item 2.02 (NOT Item 4.02 non-reliance), bundled with preliminary Q1 2026 commentary (F2 / recast-but-not-restatement). All three share one common deeper signal that a future resolver could exploit: the prior-periodic snapshot already has a periodic AT or PAST the period the new 8-K will turn out to announce. PHR has a 10-K already filed for FY2026; PINC has the Q3 FY2025 10-Q already filed (one day before); PRU has the Q4 2025 10-K already filed (Feb 2026). In every case, 'advance prior periodic by one quarter' is wrong precisely because the prior periodic is not 'prior to the 8-K's true period' — it is at or past it.
caveats: PRU truth is Tier C (no companion periodic, no consensus naming) and the 8-K is genuinely multi-period (preliminary Q1 2026 + recast Q4 2025). The audit chose Q4 2025 because EX-99.1 dominantly says '4Q25'; an alternative reading where the 8-K is 'about' Q1 2026 (matching D) is partially defensible but conflicts with the EX-99.1's primary content. This row probably belongs in a 'multi-period 8-K, audit choice ambiguous' bucket rather than a clean wrong-fire.
PRU companion fields in the fact sheet are blank, meaning the audit found no PIT-visible companion 10-Q/10-K matched to this 8-K. That is itself a structural feature, not a data gap.
PHR and PINC are unambiguously D-wrong; PRU is D-wrong-against-Tier-C-truth, which is softer.

Across all three: the audit fact sheet's `latest_per_ticker=true` field correctly flags these as the most-recent rows per ticker — but none is a 'live novel earnings event'; each is a follow-up filing whose true period is at or before the prior periodic. A resolver feature 'flag if prior periodic period_end >= 8-K filed - small_window AND prior 8-K already covered the implied period' would catch all three.

F4 (skipped quarter) does NOT apply to any of these rows. The failure-model phrasing 'issuer skipped a quarter' should be tightened to 'issuer is filing a non-advance 8-K (transcript / recast / inverted-order pair).'
